title: Foreigners' Office - How Auslaenderbehoerde Works and Official Letters
summary: The Auslaenderbehoerde (Foreigners' Office) issues all residence permits in Germany and sends legally binding letters regarding permit renewals, document requests, and deadlines.
steps: When you receive an official letter (Bescheid) from the Auslaenderbehoerde, read the deadline first.
Check if the letter requires a response, additional documents, or an in-person appointment.
If you cannot understand the German letter, seek a certified translator or legal support immediately.
Typical letters include permit renewal notice, request for missing documents, and hearing invitations.
To appeal a decision (Widerspruch), submit a written appeal within 1 month of the date on the letter.
Book an appointment via the Auslaenderbehoerde online portal; walk-ins are typically not accepted.
Attend appointment with all requested documents, bring originals and copies.
If permit is expiring, apply for renewal at least 6-8 weeks before expiry date.
documents: Employment contract or proof of income (Einkommensnachweis)
Any documents specifically requested in the letter
office: Auslaenderbehoerde (Foreigners' Office), location varies by city.
fee: EUR100 for residence permit; EUR28.80 for extension
notes: Widerspruch (appeal) must be filed within 1 month or the decision usually becomes final.
